name: Smothered Round Steak
recipeInstructions:
Smothering is a multipurpose Cajun technique that works wonders with everything from game to snap beans. It's similar to what the rest of the world knows as braising—the ingredients are briefly browned or sauteed, then cooked with a little liquid over low heat for an extended time.
1. Season the roast with half of the salt and peppers.
2. Dust the roast with flour on all sides.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or other large heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add the steak and brown well on all sides.
4. Remove the meat and pour off all but 1 teaspoon of oil.
5. Add half the onions, bell peppers, celery, and the remaining seasonings. Stir well and reduce the heat to the lowest possible point.
6. Return the roast to the pot and cover with the remaining vegetables. Cover and let cook until the meat is very tender (about 1 hour and 15 minutes).
7. Serve the meat in slices with rice alongside and gravy over all.
Adjust cooking times accordingly when using other meats; tenderness should be your guide. For extra flavorful roasts, try larding with slivers of garlic before smothering.
Serves 4-6
